claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority

Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm inquiring about my child Alex, who is 9 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're inquiring about Alex, who is 9 years old.

To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: **Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I live abroad, but Alex doesn't live with me either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I understand you live abroad and Alex doesn't live with you.

Let me ask: **Are you contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation), and if so, how much do you contribute?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep. I help with things like clothes, school expenses, and other necessities. I send about £30 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask: **Which country do you currently live in?**

This is important because eligibility for Child Benefit when living abroad depends on whether you live in certain specific countries or if you're a Crown servant.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in Spain at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information.

To help determine if you might be eligible for Child Benefit while living in Spain, I need to ask: **Are you working as a Crown servant (such as for the UK civil service, diplomatic service, or armed forces), or do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant, and I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also ask: **Who does Alex currently live with, and does that person receive Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with my ex-partner. I'm not sure if they're claiming Child Benefit or not.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex. Let me explain the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

The main requirement for Child Benefit is that you normally live in the UK. While there are some exceptions for people living abroad (such as Crown servants or those living in certain specific countries), you don't qualify under these exceptions since:

- You're not a Crown servant
- You don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Living in Spain alone doesn't qualify you under the exceptions

Additionally, Alex doesn't live with you - they live with your ex-partner. While your financial contributions of £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep show you're supporting them, this doesn't override the fundamental requirement to be living in the UK.

**It's worth noting that your ex-partner may be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex if they live in the UK and are responsible for Alex's care.** Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child, so if they're not already claiming, they should consider doing so.

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, so meets the age requirement (under 16). However, to qualify for Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK. The rules allow exceptions for people living abroad only if they live in certain countries or are Crown servants. You live in Spain and are not a Crown servant, nor do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Additionally, Alex does not live with you - they live with your ex-partner. While you contribute £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep, you do not meet the fundamental residency requirement of living in the UK or qualifying under the specific exceptions for living abroad. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in Spain (not the UK) and does not qualify under any of the exceptions for living abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme). They also do not live with their 9-year-old child Alex, who lives with the claimant's ex-partner. While they contribute financially to Alex's upkeep, they do not meet the basic residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.

The rules allow exceptions for people living abroad only if they live in certain countries or are Crown servants. You live in Spain and are not a Crown servant, nor do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Additionally, Alex does not live with you - they live with your ex-partner. While you contribute £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep, you do not meet the fundamental residency requirement of living in the UK or qualifying under the specific exceptions for living abroad. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in Spain (not the UK) and does not qualify under any of the exceptions for living abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme). They also do not live with their 9-year-old child Alex, who lives with the claimant's ex-partner. While they contribute financially to Alex's upkeep, they do not meet the basic residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.
